days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: First steps into the vibrant city

timeline.title: Arrive at Tokyo Haneda Airport
timeline.detail: After clearing customs, take the Tokyo Monorail into the city. Enjoy scenic views of Tokyo Bay during your ride.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: This hotel is famous for its Godzilla-themed room and is centrally located in Shinjuku, just a short walk from the train station.

timeline.title: Dinner at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Savor one of the best ramen experiences in Tokyo, known for its delicious tonkotsu broth and unique solo dining booths.

timeline.title: Shibuya Crossing Experience
timeline.detail: Step into one of the busiest pedestrian crossings in the world. Feel the energy of the crowd as you make your way to the other side.

timeline.title: Explore Shibuya Center Gai
timeline.detail: Stroll through this bustling alley filled with shops, restaurants, and street performers. It’s a great spot for people-watching.

localTips: Buy a Suica card for easy subway access throughout your visit.
Many restaurants in Tokyo require no tipping; check before you leave.
Be mindful of local customs, such as quietness on public transport.

days.title: Tokyo Sightseeing
days.subtitle: Temples and Culture

timeline.title: Breakfast at Tsukiji Outer Market
timeline.detail: Start your day with fresh sushi and seafood at this famous market. It’s the perfect way to taste Tokyo's renowned seafood.

timeline.title: Visit Senso-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo's oldest temple located in Asakusa. Don’t miss the iconic Kaminarimon Gate and shop for souvenirs along Nakamise Street.

timeline.title: Lunch at Asakusa Imahan
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional meal of sukiyaki at this historic restaurant known for its high-quality beef and authentic setting.

timeline.title: Explore Ueno Park
timeline.detail: Stroll through this popular park which features museums, a zoo, and cherry blossom trees. Great for a leisurely walk and cultural insight.

timeline.title: The Tokyo National Museum
timeline.detail: Discover the extensive collection of art and antiquities at Japan's oldest and largest museum. It's a deep dive into the nation's rich history.

timeline.title: Dinner at Tofuya Ukai
timeline.detail: Dine at a beautiful restaurant offering kaiseki-style dishes featuring fresh and seasonal ingredients, with a focus on their famous tofu.

localTips: Plan your temple visits early to avoid crowds.
Check if any festivals or events are happening during your stay for a richer experience.
Keep cash handy for small shops; not all accept cards.

days.title: Day Trip to Hakone
days.subtitle: Nature & Onsen Experience

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty buffet breakfast at your hotel before a day of adventure in Hakone.

timeline.title: Travel to Hakone
timeline.detail: Take the Romancecar train from Shinjuku to Hakone. Get your camera ready for stunning views along the route.

timeline.title: Hakone Open-Air Museum
timeline.detail: Explore this unique outdoor museum filled with sculptures and artwork set against a backdrop of stunning natural scenery.

timeline.title: Lunch at Hakone Yumoto
timeline.detail: Savor local specialties like soba and fresh river fish at this charming town known for its hot springs.

timeline.title: Relax at an Onsen
timeline.detail: Experience Japanese culture at an onsen (hot spring). There are many options; be sure to choose one that allows day visitors.

timeline.title: Return to Tokyo
timeline.detail: Ride the Romancecar back to Shinjuku Station to freshen up and have dinner back in the city.

timeline.title: Dinner in Omoide Yokocho
timeline.detail: End your day in this nostalgic alley filled with tiny yakitori and izakaya bars offering local food experiences.

localTips: Buy a Hakone Free Pass for unlimited travel in Hakone.
Try to visit the Owakudani Valley for views of volcanic activity.
Bring a swimsuit if you plan to soak in an outdoor onsen.

days.title: Last day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Cultural and Shopping Day

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Fuel up with a traditional Japanese breakfast at your hotel to start your final day in Tokyo.

timeline.title: Visit Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Pay your respects at this peaceful Shinto shrine surrounded by beautiful forest. It's a serene escape in the heart of Tokyo.

timeline.title: Explore Harajuku & Takeshita Street
timeline.detail: Wander through this vibrant area known for its youth culture and fashion. Sample crepes and shop at unique boutiques.

timeline.title: Lunch at Afuri Ramen
timeline.detail: Taste the unique yuzu ramen at Afuri, which is a favorite among locals and known for its fresh flavors.

timeline.title: Shopping in Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Spend your afternoon shopping at the major department stores and boutiques in Shinjuku. Great place for unique souvenirs.

timeline.title: Final dinner at Narisawa
timeline.detail: Experience a memorable dining experience at Michelin two-star Narisawa, known for their innovative Japanese cuisine. Reservation in advance is recommended.

timeline.title: Return to hotel for check-out
timeline.detail: Head back to your hotel, pack up and prepare for departure tomorrow.

localTips: Try to visit a convenience store for Japanese snacks; they are a must!
Have cash on hand as many smaller shops may not accept cards.
Make dinner reservations for popular restaurants weeks in advance to secure a spot.

hotels.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
hotels.note: Practical location in the heart of Shinjuku, Godzilla-themed hotel.
hotels.address: 1 Chome-19-1 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo, 160-0021, Japan
